title: Arrays, Slices, Backing Storage, Append, Copy, and Capacity · Runtime Contract: Slice Retention Growth
summary: A batch processor must window records, grow buffers, preserve caller data, and avoid retention or aliasing defects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go slice retention growth increment in an alias-safe batch and buffer toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Diagnose accidental large-array retention and evaluate preallocation from measured size and growth evidence.
Predict whether append reuses or replaces storage and use copy or clone patterns to establish ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Slice Retention Growth · Theory Slice Retention Growth
steps.instruction:
theory case — A batch slice exposes 8 of 12 records from shared storage. Append one result, mutate one element, clone one boundary, and predict length, capacity, aliasing, and retained storage after every operation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Slice Retention Growth contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Slice Retention Growth · Theory Slice Retention Growth.

For Predict Slice Retention Growth · Theory Slice Retention Growth, keep Slice Retention Growth connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
